page: Microsoft Azure M176bs_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MBSMediumMemoryv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 176 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 88 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8473C processor operating at 4.0 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by a 105 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.73 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable and optimized for block storage performance.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory throughput. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores rank in the top 10%, reflecting high computational density. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier performance, reaching 433.27 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 11582.48 GB/sec for cached small-block reads. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier status, with Redis handling 171.63 million operations per second. For AI workloads, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions enable strong performance in LLM inference, processing 250.75 tokens per second on medium 7B models and 36.1 tokens per second on large 70B models.

Given its high resource density and lack of local storage, the M176bs_v3 offers qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads requiring massive memory footprints without the overhead of local NVMe drives. It is designed for memory-intensive applications, large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure M176bs_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8473C processor with 176 dedicated vCPUs and 1536.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 4.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it features 88 physical cores, a 105 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. The server does not include local storage or GPUs but is designed for high-performance block storage. Benchmark data places this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis throughput reaching 171.63 million operations per second. It delivers strong performance in LLM inference and software compilation. This server is best suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale in-memory databases, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M176bs_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 176 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1.5 TB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M176bs_v3 is a memory-optimized server with 176 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1536 GB of RAM, built for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual machine delivering top-tier multi-core compute and massive memory bandwidth for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 176 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 4.0 GHz
1536.0 GB of system memory with 8.73 GB per core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Designed for Premium Storage and high-performance block storage
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis, and LLM inference
